headlines.title: Democrat moves to drop out of Nebraska’s Senate race, clearing path for independent
headlines.description: Nebraska Democratic Senate nominee Cindy Burbank has submitted a letter requesting her name be removed from the ballot, a move that would set up a one-on-one matchup between Republican Sen.

headlines.title: Wisconsin Lt. Gov. Sara Rodriguez drops out of the governor’s race
headlines.description: Her exit prompted Milwaukee County Executive David Crowley to revive his campaign and jump back into the wide-open Democratic primary in the battleground state.

headlines.title: Trump fumes over Canadian wildfire smoke and issues tariff threat
headlines.description: President Donald Trump on Friday pledged to hold Canada responsible for costs associated with unhealthy air quality in the U.S. due to spreading smoke from the Canadian wildfires.

headlines.title: Trump calls for new election security measures
headlines.description: In a primetime address to the nation President Trump castigated the security of the U.S. election system. Critics called out his speech for lacking new evidence to support his claims. NBC News’ Garrett Haake reports.

headlines.title: Maine Democrat Dr. Nirav Shah says he won’t abandon 'progressive values' in bid to replace Platner
headlines.description: Maine Democrats participated in the first debate since Graham Platner dropped out of the Senate race. Dr. Nirav Shah joined Meet the Press NOW to discuss why he’s best placed to beat Sen. Susan Collins (R) in November.

headlines.title: Americans should have ‘confidence’ in accuracy of 2020 election: Ousted election commissioner
headlines.description: NBC News correspondents break down President Trump's address to the nation on election integrity and allegations that the U.S. election system is insecure, including accusations against China. Thomas Hicks, who was recently fired as chair of the Election Assistance Commission, reacts to the president’s claims.

headlines.title: State Assemblyman Alex Bores speaks on AI and politics
headlines.description: State Assemblyman Alex Bores, a candidate for New York's 12th Congressional District, speaks with NBC News’ Gadi Schwartz about why some top artificial intelligence companies are opposing his candidacy and whether AI should play a role in politics.

headlines.title: Paul Pelosi charged with misdemeanor hit-and-run after hitting parked car
headlines.description: Pelosi, who’s married to Democratic Rep. Nancy Pelosi, previously pleaded guilty to driving under the influence in a 2022 car crash.

headlines.title: Trump speech ripple effects: From the Politics Desk
headlines.description: Welcome to From the Politics Desk, a daily newsletter that brings you the NBC News Politics team’s latest reporting and analysis from the White House, Capitol Hill and the campaign trail.

headlines.title: Trump backs Sen. Darline Graham in primary to replace her late brother
headlines.description: Trump backed Sen. Darline Graham, R-S.C., in the special election to fill her late brother’s Senate seat for a full term.

headlines.title: DHS Secretary Markwayne Mullin threatens states over elections after Trump speech
headlines.description: Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin on Friday threatened state election officials.

headlines.title: Federal panel signals it could support permanent fencing around White House park known for protests
headlines.description: A historic hub for free speech and protest is under the spotlight as the Trump administration proposes adding permanent fencing around Lafayette Square.
